Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Shelly crash: break synchronous scheduleStep recursion via Timer

During the SOLAR_CHARGING → IDLE transition the control script crashed
with "Too much recursion — the stack is about to overflow" inside
currentSchedulerView, invoked from scheduleStep → runBoundedPool →
done → scheduleStep.

Root cause: when the scheduler returns closeNow non-empty but
nextResumeAt === null (typical for a pure-close transition with
holds already satisfied), the shell previously called scheduleStep()
directly from inside the runBoundedPool callback chain. Although the
logical recursion is only one level deep, the combined stack frames —
HTTP.GET callback → runBoundedPool closure → done → runValveBatch cb
→ runValveBatch([]) (sync) → runBoundedPool (sync) → done → cb →
scheduleStep → currentSchedulerView — hit Espruino's shallow stack
limit (~10-20 frames) and tripped the runtime's "too much recursion"
guard at cur[names[i]] = !!state.valve_states[names[i]].

Fix: always re-enter scheduleStep via Timer.set so the next SCHEDULE
iteration runs on a fresh JS stack. The nextResumeAt !== null branch
already did this; the nextResumeAt === null branch now uses delay=1
ms to schedule an immediate re-evaluation. A 1 ms timer yields to
the event loop, which is long enough to reset the stack without
noticeably delaying the transition.

Verified on-device: after uploading the patched script via kubectl
exec → chunked Script.PutCode, Script.GetStatus returns
{running: true, errors: none, mem_free: ~5-7 KB} and stays healthy.
The SC → IDLE transition completes normally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Query AI platforms like real users — web search + no system prompts

This is how Peec AI, Profound, and Otterly collect accurate data:
- OpenAI: Responses API + web_search_preview (already done)
- Claude: Upgraded to claude-sonnet-4 + web_search tool (was knowledge-only)
- Gemini: google_search grounding, no system instruction

Real users don't have system prompts coaching the AI to "always give
business names." The old approach biased results and produced data
that no customer would ever see. Now every platform is queried exactly
as a real person would use it.

commit.message:
Revert to f32 kernel + add nroots/overflow guards

Reverted TASK_STRIDE from 64 (f64e) to 36 (f32) — the f64e approach
is correct but creates too much task data (3.7 GB for caffeine).

Added guards:
1. nroots ≤ 5 check in dispatcher (Chebyshev table accurate to 1e-7
   for nroots 1-5, 1.26e-4 for nroots 6)
2. n_tasks overflow check (falls back to CPU when tasks exceed 10M)

The Rys largex threshold fix (99 vs 35+5n) is preserved — this is the
critical fix that eliminates 1% weight errors for T > 45.

Status: Metal int3c2e_ip1 works correctly for:
- def2-SVP (nroots ≤ 5): benzene gradient 3.21e-6 error
- Molecules with < 10M primitive triples

Falls back to CPU for:
- def2-TZVP and larger (nroots = 6)
- Large molecules (caffeine: 25.8M triples)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix broken majority vote — RUNS_PER_QUERY 2→1, fix vote threshold

The majority vote with RUNS_PER_QUERY=2 used `mentionedCount > length/2`
which is `> 1` for 2 runs — requiring BOTH runs to agree (AND gate, not
majority). Non-deterministic AI responses meant ~50% of valid mentions
were being suppressed. Fix: single run (deterministic text matching
doesn't need averaging) and change threshold to >= 1 as safety net.
Also adds better scan logging for debugging.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: verse superscripts inside italic/bold spans in prose text

appendProseText found verse numbers first, then passed segments to appendStyledText.
This failed when a star marker appeared immediately before a digit (e.g. '*5 he
predestined*') — the star blocked the regex match, so the digit landed inside
the em as plain text, not as a superscript.

Fix: reverse processing order. Split on bold/italic markers first, then call
_appendVerseNums on each plain-text segment including content inside styled spans.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Shelly crash: replace Array.sort() with pre-sorted iteration

Post-023 deploy, the control script crashed on the Pro 4PM with:
  Uncaught Error: Function "sort" not found!
  at opening.sort(); in buildSnapshotFromState

Shelly's Espruino runtime does not implement Array.prototype.sort()
— same family of missing methods as Array.prototype.shift() that
the relayCmdQueue comment already documents. The 023 feature had
two sort() calls:

1. planValveTransition's `names.sort()` for deterministic iteration
   of the target/current key union.
2. buildSnapshotFromState's `opening.sort()` for alphabetizing the
   staged-open list.

Both are now replaced with iteration of a new pre-sorted constant
VALVE_NAMES_SORTED defined next to MODE_VALVES. All 8 valve names
are known at compile time, so no runtime sorting is needed.

Also extend the SH-014 lint rule to flag any future Array.sort() /
Array.findLastIndex() calls as errors — the rule already caught
.shift()/.unshift()/.splice()/.flat()/.flatMap()/.findLast() but
was missing sort() which is why the crash slipped through CI. The
rule's comment now records both 2026-04-09 (.shift crash) and
2026-04-10 (.sort crash) as empirical evidence.

Verified: `npm run test:unit` (406 tests pass) + shelly-lint clean
(0 errors, only pre-existing warnings). A synthetic file with a
.sort() or .findLastIndex() call now fails the lint with SH-014.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: verse superscripts in bold ALL: lines; serving intra-week split ctrls

Bug 1 — text-renderer.js: verse numbers not superscripted inside ALL: bold text.
When inAll=true, renderBodyText was using b.textContent = ev.text which bypasses
appendProseText. Inline verse numbers inside ALL: lines were rendered as plain
text inside <strong> instead of <sup>. Fix: call appendProseText(b, ev.text)
when prose=true so the INLINE_VERSE_RE scan runs inside the bold span. Same fix
applied to blockItalic case for consistency.

Bug 2 — calendar.js renderServingWeek: split controls between service-time groups
(and between flat teams) still used data-splitType='serving' (old attribute) but
the click handler was migrated to data-breakType='serving-split' in #132. Clicks
were silently dropped — no match, no action. Fix: replace both hand-assembled
split ctrls in renderServingWeek with makeSplitCtrlEl(makeBreakSrc('serving-split',
{...})) so they stamp the correct breakType and are handled by the click handler.

commit.message:
Disable f64e int3c2e_ip1: correct but task builder too slow for large mol

The f64-emulated kernel produces correct integrals (1.27e-7 max rel err
on H2O, 3.8e-6 gradient on benzene) but the Numba task builder with
TASK_STRIDE=64 creates ~1.2 GB of task data for benzene (4.6M tasks ×
64 floats), which takes too long to build and exceeds practical memory
bounds for caffeine.

The Rys largex threshold fix (99 vs 35+5n) is confirmed correct and
essential — it eliminates the 1% weight error for T > 45. This fix
applies to any future kernel that uses the Clenshaw Rys tables.

To enable f64e for production:
1. Reduce TASK_STRIDE by sharing f64 inputs across primitives in the
   same shell triple (v2 architecture: one thread per shell triple)
2. Or: use f64e only for the TRR coefficients (c0, b10, b01, b00) that
   derive from the f64 inputs, keeping the g-array in f32 — since the
   Rys roots/weights are now accurate (threshold fix), the remaining
   precision issue may be resolved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Limit concurrent valve operations — PSU + capacitor-hold enforcement

Enforces two hardware-protection rules on every mode transition:

1. At most two valves may energize from closed→open simultaneously
   (24 V PSU current budget). Further opens are queued and fired as
   opening windows end.
2. Every valve must hold open ≥60 s before being commanded to close
   (closing-motion capacitor charging time). Premature closes are
   deferred until the hold elapses.

The logic is split into a pure `planValveTransition` scheduler in
`shelly/control-logic.js` (fully unit-tested) and a SCHEDULE-loop state
machine in `shelly/control.js` that drives it via a transition-scoped
Shelly timer. Safety-critical drains honor both rules (they are
physical constraints) but add no further throttles. `stopDrain` now
routes through the same SCHEDULE loop. The playground Status and
Device views surface staged-transition progress via three new snapshot
fields: `opening`, `queued_opens`, `pending_closes`.

New pure helpers exported from `control-logic.js`:
- `VALVE_TIMING` (maxConcurrentOpens=2, openWindowMs=20000, minOpenMs=60000)
- `planValveTransition(target, current, openSince, opening, now, cfg)`
- `toSchedulerView` / `fromSchedulerView` (v_air polarity inversion)
- `runBoundedPool` (caps concurrent HTTP at 4, leaving one of the 5
  Shelly slots for telemetry + relay-command queue)
- `buildSnapshotFromState` (pure state-snapshot builder, now
  unit-testable)

Tests: +33 unit tests covering foundational scheduler, US1 slot budget,
US2 cross-tick queue, US3 min-open hold + 1000-iteration invariant fuzz,
US4 safety-drain interactions, SCHEDULE-loop integration, polarity
helpers, snapshot builder, and bounded pool. +3 e2e tests for staged-
valve DOM wiring. All existing tests still pass (400 unit + 109 e2e).

Hardware-gated tasks (T050c, T053, T054, T055 device walkthrough) remain
open. See specs/023-limit-valve-operations/tasks.md "Closing Notes" for
script-size delta (+19 KB combined) and the verified non-hardware
gotchas.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: nuke broken pyenv (detached HEAD) and re-clone fresh

Detects detached HEAD state via git symbolic-ref. If broken,
removes ~/.pyenv entirely and clones fresh from master.
No more shallow clone tag pinning.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: halo-ai-core
repository.description: Bare-metal AI platform for AMD Strix Halo. One script. Everything works. Lego blocks — snap in what you need.

commit.message:
fix: pyenv stuck on detached HEAD — checkout master before pull

Old install cloned pyenv with --depth 1 on a tag, leaving it on
detached HEAD. git pull does nothing there. Now does fetch + checkout
master + pull to get latest python-build definitions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: halo-ai-core
repository.description: Bare-metal AI platform for AMD Strix Halo. One script. Everything works. Lego blocks — snap in what you need.
